_meta.primary_authority: UniProt P02788 (TRFL_HUMAN), entry version 268, sequence version 6

mature_protein_numbering._note: Mature protein begins at UniProt position 20 after signal-peptide cleavage; subtract 19 to convert UniProt → mature.

boundary_justification.primary_source: UniProt P02788 FT DOMAIN annotations: N-lobe DOMAIN 25..352, C-lobe DOMAIN 364..695. Residues 353-363 are the inter-domain gap.
boundary_justification.structural_corroboration: PDB 1B0L (diferric hLf, 2.2 Å) shows residues 353-363 as a structured α-helix/turn segment bridging the two globular lobes. AlphaFold AF-P02788-F1 per-residue pLDDT for this segment is 93-98 (well-folded), NOT the disordered loop the brief's framing implies.
boundary_justification.structural_observation_caveat: AF pLDDT 93-98 for 353-363 contradicts the 'flexible loop' framing in wiki/lactoferrin.md §3.1 (which cites the lobe-lobe linker as a short α-helical linker). The wiki and PDB are correct — the WT linker is a STRUCTURED helix segment. The protease-resistance question is therefore not 'this loop is flexible and gets cleaved' but rather 'this exposed helix presents Arg/Lys/Ala/Val residues to bulk solvent at the inter-lobe groove, and is therefore preferentially targeted by ALP subtilisin in the koji environment.' Comp-005 confirms: 0 exposed ALP cleavage sites land in the mature-protein linker region directly (the 3 ALP exposed sites are all in surface loops elsewhere). The redesign rationale therefore shifts to PRECAUTIONARY: if any koji-environment partial unfolding occurs, the inter-lobe linker is the most plausible protease-presentation site because (a) it is geometrically exposed, (b) it contains 5 ALP-preferred residues out of 11, and (c) the RRAR cluster is a known subtilisin/trypsin-like recognition motif.

alp_preferred_residue_hits_in_wt._note: S (Ser at 353), V (Val at 357), A (Ala at 358-359, 362), R (Arg at 360-361, 363) all match ALP P1 preference. Of the 11 residues, only the 3 Glu residues (354, 355, 356) are NOT in the ALP-preferred P1 set. The linker is highly enriched in ALP-cleavable residues.

design_constraints.preserve_flanking: Residues 352 (K, end of N-lobe) and 364 (V, start of C-lobe) are FIXED — they anchor the lobe-lobe geometry.
design_constraints.preserve_length: Length=11 residues kept constant. Length changes would shift C-lobe registry relative to N-lobe and disrupt the iron-binding geometry inherited from PDB 1B0L.
design_constraints.avoid_p1_preferred_for_proteases: Strongly discourage L, F, Y, A, V, I, M, W, K, R, G, T, S at most positions (ALP P1) and L, F, I, V, M, A at the residue AFTER any potential cleavage point (NPr P1') AND F, L, Y, A, V, I as either P1 or P1' (acid protease).
design_constraints._pool_rationale: E and D are negatively charged and not in any koji-protease preferred P1; N and Q are polar uncharged; H is mildly basic but not in ALP P1; P (proline) breaks any extended chain and inhibits cleavage at P1-P1' due to its imino structure. P is the strongest single-residue protease blocker known.
